channel.title: Chroniq Now: Breaking Global News and Headlines Daily
channel.description: Chroniq Now - your trusted source for breaking global news, in-depth analysis and up-to-the-minute coverage of politics, business, culture and more.

item.title: Supreme Court Takes Up Voting Rights Case as Hegseth Faces Iran War Grilling
item.description:
The Supreme Court weighed a landmark Voting Rights Act redistricting challenge Tuesday as Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced sharp Senate scrutiny over the administration's Iran war strategy and costs.

Key points:
• The Supreme Court ruled 6–3 to strike down Louisiana's majority-Black congressional map, significantly raising the legal bar for future Voting Rights Act redistricting challenges.
• Justice Samuel Alito's majority opinion now requires plaintiffs to show strong evidence of intentional racial discrimination, a standard critics say makes VRA cases nearly impossible to win.
• Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced his first congressional hearing since the U.S.-Israel war on Iran began in February, with the Pentagon disclosing the conflict has cost approximately $25 billion so far.
• Hegseth clashed with Democratic lawmakers over the war's strategic goals and timeline, while also facing bipartisan criticism over his firing of top military officials including Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George.

Read the full article: Supreme Court Takes Up Voting Rights Case as Hegseth Faces Iran War Grilling

item.title: Driven Advisors Scrutinized: Bancrédito Holding Dispute
item.description:
Driven Advisors and Administrative Services face scrutiny in a legal dispute with Bancrédito Holding Corp, owner of Bancrédito International Bank and Trust.

Read the full article: Driven Advisors Scrutinized: Bancrédito Holding Dispute
